cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how the author introduces the topic. The options are A through D. The passage starts with several questions: "Do you start your work by making a list..." and so on. So the author is asking the reader a series of questions to engage them. Then after those questions, the author mentions that if you're a natural list maker, this habit is good, and then lists reasons why. The options are A) asking a series of questions, B) presenting some simple facts, C) stating his own point of view, D) listing the benefits of a to-do list. The introduction part here is the first paragraph, which begins with the questions. The rest of the paragraphs list the benefits. But the question is about how the author introduces the topic.
